title: Debt settlement is off — trace the symptom to the money parser
task: Two tests in test/settle.test.js fail: settling transfers produces wrong balances whenever an amount has a single-digit fraction (e.g. "5.5" is treated as $5.05 instead of $5.50). The failing behavior surfaces in src/settle.js, but find and fix the actual root cause so `npm test` passes. Do NOT modify test/settle.test.js.

checks.pattern: single-digit fractions are worth ten times the cents

notes: Symptom (wrong settlement balances) is in settle.js/netBalances, but the root cause is toMinorUnits in money.js not right-padding single-digit fractions to two places. A fix that only patches settle.js cannot satisfy the padEnd(2) check on money.js, and ad-hoc compensation upstream would still mis-handle other single-digit cases. The padEnd(2) check pins the fix to the parser.
